title: 3 Legal Considerations and Policies for Enterprise Fraud Management
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations and Policies for Enterprise Fraud Management
3.1	Policy for Setting Interac
Â®
e-Transfer Limits
Financial institutions wanting to participate in Central 1âs Limit Groups feature should do an internal risk assessment to determine which limit group they would like to participate in.
3.2	Policy on Restricting Access to Enterprise Fraud Management
Your financial institution designates its own Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) users. We recommend that you segregate duties and restrict administrative access to EFM. Make sure that access to the application reflects the usersâ current job responsibilities and authorization levels. See Section 4.2, EFM User Roles.
3.3	Policy on Internal Controls and User Management
Your financial institution is responsible for employing security practices and establishing internal controls for monitoring activity in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM). We recommend that internal controls and procedures relating to the use of EFM are established to prevent or detect errors in a timely manner.
Policies may include but arenât limited to the following:
Ensuring that EFM users have their own unique user IDs and donât share user IDs
Ensuring that EFM users receive their one-time Client Centre password in a secure and confidential manner (applies to individuals who do not already have a Client Centre username or whose Client Centre password is reset)
Deleting EFM users who
â
are inactive for one year or more,
â
no longer require access to EFM, or
â
are no longer employees of your financial institution
Ensuring that EFM users are reviewed on a regular basis and that required action is taken to update or delete profiles
3.4	Policy on Client Centre Passwords
Secure passwords are necessary to prevent unauthorized access to the computer network. See the CPS Admin Application Part or the User Management Part for characteristics and guidelines for creating secure passwords.
Two steps are required for users to access Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM). First, users log in to the Client Centre with their user ID and password. Once successfully authenticated, they will select a link to EFM on the Product Applications page. Next, a 2-step security code is required to sign in to EFM.
END
